summary: Iteration 15 (Organiser signup flow + same-powers-as-company_admin RBAC). 8/8 NEW backend tests PASS, 31/31 regression OTP/signup tests PASS (39/39 total). Full UI flow verified: nav-signup-organiser-btn (cyan #06B6D4) routes to /signup-organiser, 2-step form works, 'Any email works' hint present, OTP banner shows, OTP from organiser_signup_otps completes signup and lands user on /dashboard (per direct API: returns role=organiser, company_id, company_name; org_type=organiser tagged on companies doc; OTP marked verified=true). UI login as organiser shows nav-guide-organiser ('Organiser guide' -> /manuals/kreeda-nation-organiser-manual.pdf, HEAD 200), Dashboard/Hire/Players/Manage nav appear (company_admin parity). Footer 'Become an organiser' -> /signup-organiser. Company signup still rejects free emails (regression intact).

integration_issues.issue: After /api/auth/login as organiser, UI lands on '/' instead of '/dashboard'. The role-based redirect map on /login does not include 'organiser'. Non-blocking — /dashboard is accessible from nav and works correctly. The signup flow does land on /dashboard correctly.

action_items: (Optional) Add 'organiser' to the role -> redirect-path map in /app/frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx (or wherever post-login routing decides) so direct UI login as organiser lands on /dashboard like other company-scoped roles do.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/auth.py is now ~400 lines and still has duplicated signup logic between `company_signup` and `organiser_signup` (only difference is the corporate-email guard + org_type tag). Refactor into a shared `_create_company_scoped_account(role, otp_collection, org_type, require_corporate)` helper — same DRY improvement we flagged in iter 14 for vendor/player signup.

`_consume_signup_otp_sync` at the top of auth.py is defined but appears unused (the in-closure `_consume_signup_otp` is used everywhere). Either wire `_consume_signup_otp_sync` through `deps` so vendors.py / server.py:player_register can reuse it (the actual DRY win), or drop the dead helper. Currently we still have 3 inline OTP-consume copies.

server.py is still ~2.9K lines. Extracting events.py / bookings.py modules (which this iteration touched) into their own files would shrink the closure and make `require_company_admin`/`is_company_scoped` testable in isolation.

CompanySignupBody.otp is Optional[str] = ''. The handler then 400s if missing. Promote to required at the Pydantic schema layer so OpenAPI documents it and FastAPI's 422 takes over (consistency with other v2 endpoints).
Frontend Login.jsx post-login redirect needs `organiser` added to the role -> path map (see frontend_issues above).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_organiser_signup_otp.py (NEW — 8 tests covering OTP request/validation/lockout/full signup + RBAC + login + regression)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all required CTA/nav/footer/guide checks (login redirect quirk noted as minor)

test_credentials: admin@kreedanation.com / admin123 only. All organiser/company/user data created during tests is cleaned up in finally/teardown blocks (organiser_signup_otps, companies, events, users collections).
seed_data_creation: None persisted. Test helpers /tmp/seed_organiser.py and /tmp/cleanup_organiser.py were used only for UI tests (bypassing SendGrid throttle) and every account is removed at teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite: /app/backend/tests/test_organiser_signup_otp.py (8 tests) + test_vendor_player_otp_and_email.py (15) + test_company_signup_otp.py (16) — run all three together to validate OTP-gated signup flows. Mongo collections: organiser_signup_otps, company_signup_otps, vendor_signup_otps, player_signup_otps. Frontend testids confirmed working: org-signup-{name,admin-name,phone,email,password,request-otp} -> org-signup-otp-{banner,input,back,resend,submit}. Nav: nav-signup-organiser-btn, nav-guide-organiser. SendGrid is sometimes flaky (occasional 502) — pytest tolerates retry; for UI use the /tmp/seed_organiser.py helper to bypass SendGrid when only verifying logged-in nav/footer/dashboard behaviour. The /tmp/seed_organiser.py + /tmp/cleanup_organiser.py scripts take CLI args (mongo_url, db_name, email, password, company_name).
rca of the issue: Login redirect quirk: /app/frontend/src/context/AuthContext.jsx exposes isCompanyAdmin=true for organisers, but the post-login redirect in Login.jsx likely checks the literal role string 'company_admin' to decide /dashboard. Result: organiser lands on '/'. Reproduction: seed organiser via /tmp/seed_organiser.py, visit /login, sign in -> lands on '/'. Click 'Dashboard' in nav -> works correctly. Mitigation: add 'organiser' to the role map (or check isCompanyAdmin instead of role==='company_admin').
